# Go-To-Market Strategy — Conductor

**Status:** MISSING from source → Fully Generated (⚡)  
**Source:** None (critical gap — generated by review board)  
**Last Updated:** June 2026

---

## Purpose
Defines how Conductor reaches, acquires, activates, and retains customers. GTM strategy is a P0 gap — without it, the platform has no customer acquisition path.

---

## GTM Thesis

> **Start narrow, go deep. Own one vertical, one city, 100 customers before scaling.**

The biggest GTM risk for Conductor is spreading across 5 verticals with no marketing budget and no product-market fit signal. The correct approach is:

1. Pick one vertical (Healthcare recommended)
2. Build 5 case studies with measurable outcomes
3. Use those case studies to expand to adjacent customers (same vertical, new cities)
4. Use proven vertical playbook to launch second vertical
5. Repeat

---

## Phase 1: Vertical Beachhead (Months 1–6)

### Target: Healthcare in Hyderabad/Bengaluru

**Why Healthcare:**
- High pain (no-shows are expensive, manual reminder systems break)
- Measurable ROI (attendance rate % is an objective metric)
- Word-of-mouth dense network (doctors talk to doctors)
- Low competitive density (WATI/AiSensy don't have healthcare packs)
- Initial connector needed: Google Calendar + Razorpay (simple vs. Shopify)

**Month 1–2 Goal:** 10 paying healthcare clinics  
**Month 3–4 Goal:** 30 paying healthcare clinics  
**Month 5–6 Goal:** 50 paying healthcare clinics + 2 publishable case studies

### Acquisition Channels (Phase 1)

**Channel 1: Direct Outreach (Founder-Led)**
- Target: 200 clinics in Hyderabad + Bengaluru
- Method: Founder personal outreach via LinkedIn, local medical associations, clinic WhatsApp groups
- Offer: Free 30-day setup + 3-month discounted plan in exchange for case study
- Target: 10 customers from 200 outreach attempts (5% conversion)

**Channel 2: Healthcare Vertical Communities**
- Medical association forums
- IMA (Indian Medical Association) local chapters
- Healthcare professional WhatsApp groups
- Doctor networking apps (Doceree, Docplexus)

**Channel 3: Healthcare SaaS Partnerships**
- Clinic management software (Practo, eHospital, Marg ERP)
- Approach as integration partner
- Co-marketing to their customer base

**Channel 4: Content (Long-tail)**
- Blog: "How to reduce patient no-shows by 30% using WhatsApp automation"
- YouTube: 2-minute clinic setup walkthrough videos
- SEO target: "WhatsApp appointment reminder for clinic", "patient reminder software India"

---

## Phase 2: Vertical Expansion (Months 7–18)

### Expand to: E-Commerce + Professional Services

**E-Commerce Entry:**
- Shopify App Store listing (free to list, high discovery intent)
- WooCommerce marketplace listing
- Target: D2C brands in fashion, health, home
- Content: "Recover abandoned carts via WhatsApp — case study"
- Partnership: Shopify agency partners

**Professional Services Entry:**
- LinkedIn targeting (CA, law, consulting firm owners)
- ICAI (chartered accountant) forums and groups
- Content: "Automate client follow-up and payment collection"
- Partnership: CA practice management software

### Channel Expansion (Phase 2)
- **Paid acquisition:** Meta Ads (irony: WhatsApp ads promoting WhatsApp automation), Google Search
- **Agency channel:** Onboard 10 digital marketing agencies as resellers
- **Product-led growth:** In-product referral program (₹1,000 account credit per referral)

---

## Product-Led Growth Strategy

### Free Trial Design
- 14-day full-featured trial (Growth plan)
- Activation milestones (shown in-product):
  1. WA number connected (Day 1)
  2. First workflow live (Day 2)
  3. First automated message sent (Day 3)
  4. First campaign launched (Day 7)
- Users who complete all 4 milestones within 7 days have >60% trial-to-paid conversion ⚡

### Activation Playbook
**Day 0:** Signup → "Choose your industry" → Pre-loaded industry templates
**Day 1:** Connect WhatsApp number (guided 5-step flow)
**Day 2:** Activate your first automation (1-click from template library)
**Day 3:** See your first automated message in the dashboard
**Day 7:** "Your automation has sent X messages this week" celebration email
**Day 12:** Upgrade prompt: "You're approaching your trial message limit"
**Day 14:** Trial end → Upgrade or data freeze

### Viral Loop ⚡
Every automated WhatsApp message has an optional footer: *"Powered by Conductor — automate your business"*  
- Customers can opt out (Business plan+)
- This creates B2B2C brand exposure at scale

---

## Positioning & Messaging

### Primary Message (Healthcare)
> "Your patients forget appointments. We make sure they don't. Set up WhatsApp reminders in 30 minutes — no developer, no IT team."

### Primary Message (E-Commerce)
> "68% of carts are abandoned. WhatsApp recovery messages convert 15% of them. Set up in 1 hour."

### Primary Message (General)
> "Your business runs on WhatsApp. Conductor automates it."

### Tagline Options ⚡
- "Automate your business. Not just your messages."
- "The operating system for SMB customer engagement."
- "Business automation for the WhatsApp generation."

---

## Partner Strategy

### Tier 1: Integration Partners (Technical)
Companies whose products Conductor integrates with. Goal: Listed on their marketplace.
- Shopify, WooCommerce, Razorpay, Zoho, Google Calendar, Freshdesk, Tally

### Tier 2: Distribution Partners (Commercial)
Companies that sell or recommend Conductor to their customer base.
- Digital marketing agencies
- WhatsApp BSPs (Gupshup, Kaleyra as distribution, not as competitors)
- Vertical SaaS platforms (practice management, retail POS)

### Tier 3: ISV/Marketplace Partners (Ecosystem)
Companies that build on top of Conductor's platform.
- Build connector packs (e.g., ERPNext connector)
- Build vertical packs (e.g., "Restaurant pack")
- Revenue share: 70% partner / 30% Conductor

---

## Customer Success & Retention

### Onboarding (Growth customers)
- Session 1 (Day 1): Account setup + WA connection
- Session 2 (Day 3): First workflow activation
- Session 3 (Day 14): First results review
- Ongoing: Monthly business review (automated for Growth, personal for Business+)

### Churn Prevention
- Usage-based alerts: "You haven't run a workflow in 7 days"
- Proactive reach-out at 50% message limit (upsell opportunity)
- Workflow performance alerts: "Your cart recovery workflow is converting at 12% — here's how to improve"
- Quarterly business review for Business+ customers

### Expansion Revenue
- Upsell triggers: Approaching message limit, requesting features on higher plans
- Cross-sell: Additional vertical packs, premium connectors
- Target: Net Revenue Retention >110% by Year 2

---

## Launch Strategy

### Soft Launch (Month 1-2): 10 Design Partners
- Recruit 10 healthcare clinics as design partners
- Free platform access in exchange for weekly feedback sessions
- Co-create the "Healthcare Pack" with real users
- Goal: Product-market fit signal before spending on marketing

### Private Beta (Month 3-4): 50 customers
- Open to healthcare + 1 other vertical
- Invitation only via community/referral
- Marketing: None — purely organic + direct outreach

### Public Launch (Month 5-6)
- Press release + industry media (Economic Times, YourStory, Inc42)
- Product Hunt launch
- SEO-optimized blog posts and case studies
- Activate paid ads (₹50,000/month budget)

---

## GTM Budget (Year 1) ⚡

| Category | Monthly Budget | Annual |
|---|---|---|
| Content creation | ₹30,000 | ₹3,60,000 |
| Paid ads (Phase 2+) | ₹50,000 | ₹3,00,000 (6 months) |
| Events + community | ₹20,000 | ₹2,40,000 |
| Partner program | ₹15,000 | ₹1,80,000 |
| Tools (CRM, analytics) | ₹10,000 | ₹1,20,000 |
| **Total** | **₹1,25,000** | **₹12,00,000** |
